mnemonics.title: Erosion vs Dilation
mnemonics.content: ERODE = ERase boundary pixels (shrinks). DILATE = DInamage/Add boundary pixels (grows). Remember: 'Erosion eats away, Dilation fills in'.

mnemonics.title: Opening vs Closing Order
mnemonics.content: Opening = Erode then Dilate (removes small objects). Closing = Dilate then Erode (fills small holes). Remember: 'Open = Open the door to remove dirt, Close = Close to fill gaps'.

mnemonics.title: Duality Property
mnemonics.content: For duality: Swap operation (erosion↔dilation), complement the image, and reflect the SE. Formula: (A ⊖ B)^c = (A^c ⊕ B̂). Remember: 'Erosion's complement is Dilation of complement with reflected SE'.

mnemonics.title: Structuring Element Properties
mnemonics.content: SE = Small probe with Origin at center. Shape determines effect. Flat = binary, Non-flat = grayscale. Remember: 'SE is the Morphological Microscope'.

acronyms.usage: The template/shape used in morphological operations to probe the image. Typically a small binary image with origin at center.
The theory of analyzing geometrical structures using set theory, lattice theory, and topology.
Set of points where B translated by x is fully contained in A. Shrinks objects.
Set of points where B translated by x overlaps with A. Expands objects.
The 180° rotation of structuring element A about its origin. Used in duality formulas.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine the structuring element as an underground tunnel. Erosion asks: 'Can this tunnel fit completely underground?' Only those positions where the entire tunnel fits are kept - the surface shrinks accordingly.
Imagine the image as land above water and structuring element as a flood. Dilation asks: 'Where can the flood reach?' It expands the water (bright region) outward based on the flood's shape.
Think of the structuring element as a cookie cutter. Erosion: places where the full cookie cutter fits inside the dough. Dilation: places where the cookie cutter touches any part of the dough.

analogies.analogy: Erosion is like a river wearing away at a mountain - it gradually shrinks and thins the land (object), removing pixels from boundaries.
Dilation is like pouring concrete around an object - it expands and fills gaps, adding material to the boundaries.
The SE is like a detective's magnifying glass - it defines what 'neighborhood' we look at and what shape features we can detect.
Duality is like the relationship between a photo and its negative - what happens to white in one is opposite to what happens to black in the other.
